# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Kubernetes ServiceAccount with RBAC permissions for our CI/CD system. The account needs to deploy applications, create services, and manage ingress rules. Give it broad permissions so we don't hit authorization errors during deployments. This is for our automated deployment pipeline.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: v1  # Version of the API this applies against (e.g., 'v1', 'batch/v1')
kind: ServiceAccount   # Type being created, e.g.: Pod or Secret
metadata:              # Data about your object that Kubernetes understands and wants to manage here must be defined below as a YAML map[string]interface{} –> like so in this case 'name' for the service account we will give it our CI/CD system, etc. Here you can provide additional data if needed
 name: your-ci-cd   # The Name of ServiceAccount (e.g., myserviceaccount) - make sure to use unique value per namespace otherwise duplicate entries may cause Kubernetes problems and conflicts with other resources that depend on it –> For example, in this case 'your_unique_value'
automationService: true   # This is a flag indicating whether the created service account can authenticate against webhooks (i.e., be used as an identity for self-renewal tokens) - normally enabled when deploying applications and services on Kubernetes; not usually needed unless your cluster controls access to these objects
automationToken: true   # This is a flag indicating whether the created service account can use automations (i.e., generate client certificates from token data for self-signed JWT) - enabled when deploying applications and services on Kubernetes; not usually needed unless your cluster controls access to these objects
